snakeATAC.utility 1.0.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • *snakeATAC.utility* overview

filter.hocomoco.jaspar

Source: R/filter.hocomoco.jaspar.R
filter.hocomoco.jaspar.Rd

Filter HOCOMOCO (v12+) motifs from a JASPAR-format file Reads a HOCOMOCO JASPAR-format motif file and returns the subset matching the requested transcription factors and/or motif-quality criteria. Motifs can be selected by gene symbol (bridged through the HOCOMOCO annotation, because a motif-ID prefix is a UniProt mnemonic and not always the HGNC symbol – e.g. ANDR -> AR, AP2A -> TFAP2A, SRBP2 -> SREBF2) and/or by exact HOCOMOCO motif IDs, and can be further restricted by model subtype, quality grade, and experiment type. Kept motifs are returned as a list of universalmotif objects and, if out_file is supplied, written out with universalmotif::write_jaspar().

filter.hocomoco.jaspar(
  jaspar_file,
  genes = NULL,
  motif_ids = NULL,
  subtypes = NULL,
  qualities = NULL,
  experiments = NULL,
  annotation_file = NULL,
  names_as = c("motif", "gene"),
  gene_sep = "::",
  out_file = NULL,
  overwrite = TRUE
)

Arguments

jaspar_file

Path to the HOCOMOCO JASPAR-format file (e.g. "H14CORE_jaspar_format.txt").

genes

Character vector of gene symbols to keep (case-insensitive). Requires annotation_file. A motif is kept if any of its associated genes matches.

motif_ids

Character vector of exact HOCOMOCO motif IDs to keep.

subtypes

Character/numeric vector of model subtypes to keep (e.g. 0). NULL (default) keeps all.

qualities

Character vector of quality grades to keep (e.g. c("A", "B")). NULL (default) keeps all.

experiments

Character vector of experiment codes to keep, matched exactly on the field (e.g. "P", "PSM"). NULL keeps all.

annotation_file

Path to the HOCOMOCO annotation JSONL (e.g. "H14CORE_annotation.jsonl"). Required when genes is given or names_as = "gene".

names_as

Name the returned/exported motifs by "motif" ID (default) or by "gene" symbol. The motif ID is always retained in the altname slot.

gene_sep

Separator used to join symbols when a motif maps to several genes and names_as = "gene" (default "::").

out_file

Optional output path; if supplied the filtered motifs are written there in JASPAR format.

overwrite

Passed to universalmotif::write_jaspar(). Default: TRUE.

Value

A list of universalmotif objects (the kept motifs), returned invisibly when out_file is written and visibly otherwise.

HOCOMOCO v14 motif-ID anatomy

A v14 ID such as ANDR.H14CORE.0.P.B splits (from the right) into quality (B; grades A-D, A best), experiment (P = ChIP-seq, S = HT-SELEX, M = Methyl-HT-SELEX, or combinations such as PSM), subtype (0; 0 is the primary model) and collection (H14CORE), with the TF mnemonic in front. The familiar v11 shorthand "0.A" therefore corresponds here to subtypes = 0, qualities = "A" – the experiment letters sit in between, so "0.A" is not a literal substring of the v14 ID.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
jf <- "H14CORE_jaspar_format.txt"
af <- "H14CORE_annotation.jsonl"

# All motifs for a set of genes, primary model at top quality only:
filter.hocomoco.jaspar(jf, genes = c("CTCF", "GATA1", "AR"),
                       subtypes = 0, qualities = c("A", "B"),
                       annotation_file = af)

# Specific motif IDs, renamed to gene symbols, exported to disk:
filter.hocomoco.jaspar(jf, motif_ids = "CTCF.H14CORE.0.PSM.A",
                       annotation_file = af, names_as = "gene",
                       out_file = "selected.jaspar")
} # }
